text:
There once was a small town that was famous for its annual berry festival. Every year, the town would decorate the streets with colorful berries, organize parades, and host competitions for pie eating and berry picking challenges.

This year, however, something unanticipated happened. All the berries were unusually shiny—like they’d been dipped in reflective gel. The townspeople stared as squirrels did somersaults through the shiny berries. Then, a woman in her early sixties walked into the town square. She noticed the squirrels and something else—those darned shiny berries.

Curiosity getting the better of her, she plucked a shiny berry. As she bit into it, the color spilled on her tongue—it was pure glitter! The entire town gasped. Not just at the glimmer—it was glitter-infused. Everyone had shiny berries now!

She ran for her life, singing about sparkling squirrels and glitter-infested berries, and somehow managed to turn the townspeople into glitter enthusiasts
